arts.ttl: Serie A | Milan v Juventus: Probable starting lineups
arts.sum: As Milan and Juventus prepare for their crucial Serie A encounter at San Siro, both teams have navigated the international break with varying degrees of injury challenges. Milan emerges relatively unscathed, with long-term absentees Alessandro Florenzi, Ismael Bennacer, and Matteo Gabbia remaining the only players unavailable. A potential concern arose with Theo Hernandez’s minor knee issue during France duty, but the left-back has been declared fit for Saturday’s match. Juventus, however, face a more significant injury crisis. Star striker Dusan Vlahovic returned from international duty with Serbia carrying a knock, further depleting an already stretched squad. arts.ttl: Alex Sandro: “This is a young Juve, not like mine. Weah said he felt like a striker…”
arts.sum: Former Juventus defender Alex Sandro, who left the club in June to return to Brazil, shared his thoughts on the current state of the Bianconeri and their upcoming match against AC Milan. Speaking to La Gazzetta dello Sport from Rio de Janeiro, where he now plays for Flamengo, Sandro reminisced about his time in Turin and offered insights on the new-look Juventus team. “The matches against Milan were my favourites. Partly because it’s an Italian classic and partly because the Rossoneri have always had a Brazilian tradition,” Sandro said, reflecting on his nine years and 18 encounters with Milan as The post Alex Sandro: “This is a young Juve, not like mine. arts.ttl: Weston McKennie could start in attack for Juventus against Milan
arts.sum: Ahead of the crucial Milan-Juventus clash at San Siro, manager Thiago Motta is considering an unexpected tactical twist by potentially deploying Weston McKennie as a ‘false nine’, challenging pre-match expectations. Sky Sport Italia report how with leading striker Dusan Vlahovic sidelined due to a minor injury sustained during international duty and Arkadiusz Milik also unavailable, Motta faces a significant selection challenge. arts.ttl: Sacchi: “Serie A is evolving. Inter and Atalanta ahead of Juventus”
arts.sum: The race for the Scudetto is heating up, with Inter and Atalanta attempting to leap to the top of the table, at least for one night, while Napoli awaits Roma’s visit tomorrow. Juventus also have an opportunity, but they face Milan at San Siro with several absences. Arrigo Sacchi, the renowned Italian football manager, shared his insights on the title chase and the evolution of Serie A in an interview with La Gazzetta dello Sport.
